feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
entry.content: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.

entry.title: Pinterest bets on creators with Amazon Storefront integration
entry.content: Pinterest is adding support for Amazon Storefronts, allowing creators to earn affiliate commissions more easily while showcasing their product recommendations in one place.

entry.title: Cracker Barrel Jumps Into A Short Squeeze On Upgrade, Target Hikes
entry.content: Cracker Barrel Jumps Into A Short Squeeze On Upgrade, Target Hikes

entry.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
entry.content: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or for training AI models.

entry.title: SpaceX IPO To Boost U.S. Dollar, University Endowments. Valuation Takes A Hit.
entry.content: SpaceX IPO To Boost U.S. Dollar, University Endowments. Valuation Takes A Hit.

entry.title: ServiceNow tells customers a bug left some of their data exposed to the internet
entry.content: ServiceNow is used by thousands of enterprises to automate their internal processes, but says several customers had data accessed because of a security bug.

entry.title: Inflation rises again, driven by energy costs amid Iran war
entry.content: Prices rose 4.2% for the year ending in May as inflation hit a three-year high, the government said Wednesday in a report that underscored the impact of war-related shocks to energy costs.

entry.title: Jedify raises $24M to help companies arm AI agents with context on their business
entry.content: The funding round was led by Norwest, with participation S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, and Oceans Ventures. Snowflake Ventures also participated as a strategic investor.

entry.title: Khloé Kardashian Says Daughter True Thompson Has an 'Elevated' Way of Wearing Her XO Blue Perfume
entry.content: Khloé Kardashian says daughter True Thompson, 8, is a beauty lover in the making while celerbating the launch of her XO Blue perfume.

entry.title: Decart’s new world model can simulate hours of photorealistic driving — with some caveats
entry.content: Decart is launching Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers to build on.

entry.title: A glossary to help understand what happens in an initial public offering
entry.content: Anyone following SpaceX's plans to sell shares to the public is likely to hear terms thrown around that describe steps and components of an initial public offering. Here's a quick guide.

entry.title: Snapchat limits users under 16 to sharing Spotlights with friends
entry.content: Users under 16 years old will get a separate profile to show Stories and Spotlight posts to friends that they follow back.

entry.title: Why everyone’s an energy company now
entry.content: Electricity demand from AI data centers is pushing everyone — including automakers like GM and Ford — into the energy storage business.

entry.title: Inflation hits 3-year high on spike in prices, highlighting affordability challenges for Americans
entry.content: Rising gas prices pushed inflation to its highest level in three years last month, a headache for the Federal Reserve and a potential political challenge for the Trump administration as midterm elections near.

entry.title: Why two SpaceX alumni are betting on solar and batteries to power the AI craze
entry.content: Ambrosia Energy wants to build power plants in less than 12 months while undercutting natural gas. It hopes to build gigawatts worth by 2030.

entry.title: What Are Graham Platner's Chances of Unseating Susan Collins?
entry.content: What Are Graham Platner's Chances of Unseating Susan Collins?

entry.title: Technical Assessment: Bullish in the Intermediate-Term
entry.content: Technical Assessment: Bullish in the Intermediate-Term

entry.title: US Strikes Iran in Retaliation of Downed Apache Helicopter
entry.content: The United States announced it retaliated to Iran’s downing of a U.S. Army Apache helicopter by attacking Iranian air defense, ground control stations and surveillance radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz. Iran claims it returned fired with 21 attacks against U.S. military targets across the Middle East. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

entry.title: Inflation Is Picking Investors’ Pockets
entry.content: Plus, an exodus from tech stocks

entry.title: Bugs and black mold: What some mobile home park residents see after investors buy in
entry.content: In recent years, mobile home properties have become less affordable as an influx of large companies have bought up the parks and raised rents and fees.

entry.title: Why the Supreme Court Is Debating Which Founding Fathers Were Drunks
entry.content: Because of the triumph of originalism, the court’s docket increasingly features bizarre historical arguments.

entry.title: Trump Regulator Proposes New Rules on What’s Allowed on Prediction Markets
entry.content: The CFTC will provide clearer parameters around what bets are allowed on Kalshi and other platforms, but won’t ban certain contracts.

entry.title: Now Is a Good Time to Buy Into America’s Mega Utility Merger
entry.content: The largest U.S. utility is about to buy Dominion Energy, a big peer with data-center exposure. What’s not to like?

entry.title: Anti-Nvidia Data Center Startup Is Valued at $1.55 Billion in New Funding Round
entry.content: TensorWave will use a fresh $350 million to fill more data centers with chips from AMD, an investor.

entry.title: Waymo says it built a better benchmark for comparing robotaxis to humans
entry.content: Waymo created a new computer model to help it better understand how humans behave in crash scenarios that its robotaxis encounter.

entry.title: Charli XCX tickets: How to buy, prices, concert schedule for 2026 'Music, Fashion, Film' Tour
entry.content: How to buy tickets to see Charli XCX this year with ticket prices and a schedule of 'Music, Fashion, Film' Tour.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama-Jalen Brunson controversy grows as Draymond Green brings Steph Curry into debate
entry.content: Draymond Green reacts to Victor Wembanyama's shove on Jalen Brunson.

entry.title: Get tickets to Phoebe Bridgers concerts: Prices, dates and cities for 'The Lost Tour' 2026
entry.content: Get tickets to Phoebe Bridgers concerts: See the latest pricing and a full schedule of 'The Lost Tour' in 2026.

entry.title: StubHub promo codes: How to get ticket discounts, best prices to FIFA World Cup matches this summer
entry.content: How to get ticketing promo codes, cheapest prices and more for matches at the FIFA World Cup.

entry.title: When is the World Cup opening ceremony 2026? Time, dates, performers and more to know
entry.content: Here's everything to know about this year's World Cup opening ceremony.

entry.title: Meta signs first AI data center deal in India with Reliance
entry.content: The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time.

entry.title: What channel is Knicks vs. Spurs on today? Time, schedule, live stream to watch Game 4 of 2026 NBA Finals
entry.content: Here’s everything you need to know to watch Game 4 of the Knicks vs. Spurs series, including the start time, TV channel and live stream information.

entry.title: Where to watch NCAA track and field championships: Event schedule, TV coverage, live streams and more for 2026
entry.content: Here is everything you need to know about the 2026 NCAA Track and Field Championships, including the schedule of events along with television and streaming options.

entry.title: A's light up Las Vegas in first Sin City win in 30 Years
entry.content: A's win their first MLB game in Las Vegas since April 1996.

entry.title: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to face off against Democrat Aaron Ford in Nevada’s race for governor
entry.content: Nevada Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democrat Aaron Ford have won their primaries, NBC News projects, officially setting up what’s expected to be one of the most competitive gubernatorial contests in the fall

entry.title: Graham Platner Wins Maine Primary Despite Controversies
entry.content: The Democrat will face GOP incumbent Susan Collins this fall in a crucial Senate contest.

entry.title: Top Lucid Motors executive departs amid new CEO’s leadership shakeup
entry.content: The exec, Emad Dlala, has left just a few months after being promoted to SVP of engineering and digital, TechCrunch has learned.

entry.title: Hiker who died in Runyon Canyon ID'd as Hollywood screenwriter, pal of Caitlyn Jenner
entry.content: Authorities have identified the hiker who suffered a fatal medical emergency in Runyon Canyon as 78-year-old screenwriter William Hasley, who helped Caitlyn Jenner write a motivational book.

entry.title: Steyer’s exit from the California governor’s race could spell bad news for climate policy
entry.content: Steyer’s exit from the California governor’s race could spell bad news for climate policy

entry.title: 'Michael' hits streamers, $900-million mark. 'The Verdict' also draws viewers, and threats
entry.content: As biopic 'Michael' crosses $900 million at the global box office, Netflix's molestation trial docuseries 'The Verdict' rises, and a prosecutor reports threats from Michael Jackson fans.

entry.title: Texas teen who fatally stabbed track athlete at school meet found guilty and sentenced to prison
entry.content: A Texas teenager who fatally stabbed a 17-year-old track athlete from a rival team during a high school meet was convicted of murder and sentenced to 35 years in prison on Tuesday in a case that drew wide attention beyond the booming Dallas suburb where they were students.

entry.title: Key test for Democrats in Maine
entry.content: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is poised to win a primary election tonight as he’s facing a series of scandals. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

entry.title: Trump-backed Pamela Evette advances to GOP primary runoff against Alan Wilson in South Carolina governor’s race
entry.content: NBC News projects that the state’s lieutenant governor and attorney general will face off June 23 after no candidate won more than 50% of the vote.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Nothing great was ever achieved without enthusiasm.
Lucky Numbers: 70, 11, 60, 98, 78, 56

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Fold and store bedding inside a pillowcase.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: More than 50% of the people in the world have never made or received a telephone call.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — J.R.R. Tolkien
entry.content: “Short cuts make long delays.” — J.R.R. Tolkien

entry.title: San Francisco Rejects a Tax Hike on Companies With Highly Paid Executives
entry.content: The result is a victory for Mayor Daniel Lurie and the city’s moderate Democrats.

entry.title: New Mexico attorney general calls out lack of DOJ cooperation in Epstein ranch investigation
entry.content: New Mexico attorney general calls out lack of DOJ cooperation in Epstein ranch investigation

entry.title: Motorists sue Westchester County over 1.6 billion license plate scans
entry.content: Civil rights groups have sued on behalf of New York residents to stop Westchester County, New York, from using nearly the nearly 600 license plate readers they have installed around the county

entry.title: Dudamel's Disney Hall send-off became more than a mere party
entry.content: Gustavo Dudamel's final concerts at Disney Hall as L.A. Phil music and artistic director included send-offs from John Williams and John Adams.

entry.title: Chili's, Waffle House and more: This World Cup fan is on the ultimate American road trip from Tennessee to Houston
entry.content: One World Cup fan avoided the tourist attractions to experience quintessential America for the World Cup, from Waffle House in the middle of the night to watching the NBA Finals at Chili's.

entry.title: Karmelo Anthony murder trial verdict, explained: What to know about 2025 stabbing at high school track meet
entry.content: A year after a fatal stabbing at a high school track meet, a suspect was convicted of murder on Tuesday.

entry.title: Novig Promo Code TSNEWS50: Get $50 in Novig Coins for MLB Predictions This Week
entry.content: New users can sign up with the Novig promo code TSNEWS50 and gain $50 in Novig Coins for sports prediction markets.

entry.title: Kalshi Promo Code TSNEWS: Get $10 Trade Bonus for MLB Games
entry.content: Sign up using the Kalshi promo code TSNEWS and make $10 in trades on MLB games to score a $10 prediction bonus.

entry.title: How Kobe Bryant was instrumental for Knicks & Jalen Brunson's NBA Finals run
entry.content: Jalen Brunson has been embodying Lakers legend Kobe Bryant and his Mamba Mentality which got the Knicks up 2-1 over the Spurs in the NBA Finals.

entry.title: Dusty May coaching link opens the door for Mavericks to land their own Victor Wembanyama stopper
entry.content: Mavericks eye Michigan’s Dusty May for head coach, opening a path to draft 7-foot-3 center Aday Mara to counter players like Victor Wembanyama.

entry.title: Mavs' best-case Kyrie Irving trade scenario lands them an NBA All-Star sharpshooter
entry.content: A new NBA trade idea explores the Mavericks sending Kyrie Irving to the Heat for Tyler Herro to build around rookie star Cooper Flagg.

entry.title: Consecutive series losses cost the Rays their spot in the top five
entry.content: The Yankees are right on their heels.

entry.title: U.S. launches self-sefense strikes against Iran in response to chopper downing, CENTCOM says
entry.content: The U.S. military says it began retaliatory strikes on Iran in response to the downing of an Apache helicopter. CBS News' Eleanor Watson has more.

entry.title: U.S. begins retaliatory strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter downed near Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: The U.S. has begun retaliatory strikes after Iran downed an Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, according to Central Command. "The mission is a proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression,” CENTCOM wrote in a post on X.

entry.title: House passes bill to fund ICE for rest of Trump's term after monthslong impasse
entry.content: The House passed Republicans' $70 billion bill to fund immigration enforcement agencies for the rest of the Trump administration.

entry.title: FEMA overhaul would make disaster aid harder to access, analysts warn
entry.content: A new analysis warns a proposed FEMA overhaul by a Trump-appointed panel would limit access to disaster aid for survivors, shifting the burden to state and local agencies amid hurricane season.

entry.title: Republicans pass bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of Trump’s term
entry.content: The legislation, passed only with Republican votes after Democrats demanded reforms to the immigration agencies, now heads to President Trump for his signature.

entry.title: New sunscreen option wins FDA approval after years of delay
entry.content: A sunscreen ingredient that's been available in Europe, Japan and South Korea for years has finally been approved by the FDA for sale in the U.S.

entry.title: Drone boat helps rescue U.S. Army helicopter crew shot down near Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: An unmanned surface vessel -- or drone boat -- helped rescue two Army crew members whose AH-64 Apache helicopter was shot down near the Strait of Hormuz late Monday, according to government officials and defense industry sources.

entry.title: Jury reaches guilty verdict in Karmelo Anthony murder trial
entry.content: Karmelo Anthony, 19, was found guilty of the 2025 murder of fellow high school student Austin Metcalf at a district-wide meet in Frisco, a Dallas suburb. Anthony’s legal team argued he acted in self-defense after he’d sat in the bleachers under the tent of rival high school Memorial and was confronted by members of its track team and told to leave.

entry.title: Karmelo Anthony convicted of murder in Texas track meet stabbing
entry.content: A Texas jury on Tuesday found Karmelo Anthony guilty of murder in the fatal stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a Frisco high school track meet last year, concluding a closely watched trial that drew national attention over questions of race, self-defense and school safety.

entry.title: Peru race tightens again amid market volatility as overseas, rural ballots pour in
entry.content: Peru race tightens again amid market volatility as overseas, rural ballots pour in

entry.title: Boy, bye: Ariana Grande and Ethan Slater reportedly broke up several months ago
entry.content: 'Wicked' co-stars Ariana Grande and Ethan Slater have reportedly split after three years together. The 'quiet breakup' happened 'several months ago.'

entry.title: Longtime Epstein assistant paints late sex offender as master manipulator and denies knowing about his crimes
entry.content: Longtime Epstein assistant paints late sex offender as master manipulator and denies knowing about his crimes

entry.title: Spotify accused of using new streaming policy to reduce indie artist's royalties
entry.content: Mark Kratter, a Connecticut-based musician, is suing Spotify for engaging in what he alleges are "unfair business practices." The complaint claims that streaming metrics have been altered to prioritize major artists and labels above smaller indie musicians.

entry.title: Kroger to pay $1.25 million over alleged mislabeling of calorie content of bread products
entry.content: Kroger is paying $1.25 million to settle a lawsuit from officials in California who alleged the calorie counts on certain Kroger breads were incorrect.

entry.title: Kalshi trading in 'perps' crosses $1 billion in volume within a week of launch
entry.content: A spokesperson for the company said that the product is the fastest growing in the prediction market platform's history.

entry.title: Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without a proper license between 2009 and 2025
entry.content: A former Air Canada pilot has been charged after flying for years without a proper license, Canadian police said Tuesday.

entry.title: Modest Mouse deals with death and breathes life into a new album that 'actually is ours'
entry.content: Three decades after Modest Mouse's scrappy Northwest beginnings, Isaac Brock returns with "An Eraser And A Maze," a new album he says finally feels wholly theirs.

entry.title: Questlove's Earth, Wind & Fire doc shows how trauma lingered behind a band's relentless positivity
entry.content: Drummer, DJ and director Ahmir "Questlove" Thompson talks about how the funky but aspirational band was much more than its biggest hit; plus, three other documentaries to watch in June.

entry.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
entry.content: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

entry.title: U.S. Apache helicopter shot down by Iran, Trump says; crew rescued by sea drone
entry.content: The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone in the first such operation ever carried out by the U.S. military, officials told CBS News.

entry.title: When should you repair an AC unit instead of buying a new one?
entry.content: Repairing your AC can save you thousands of dollars, but only if you know when fixing it makes financial sense.

entry.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
entry.content: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

entry.title: Trump says U.S. must respond after Apache helicopter attack over Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: President Trump said on social media that the U.S. "must respond" to the attack on an Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean has more.

entry.title: Trump says Iran shot down U.S. Apache helicopter, crew rescued by sea drone
entry.content: President Trump is vowing to respond after confirming that Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone, the first rescue of its kind. CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

entry.title: Steve Martin, Ann Philbin to curate new Santa Barbara Museum of Art exhibit of 'peculiar' paintings
entry.content: 'Martin Mull: The Joys of Indoor/Outdoor Living' in Santa Barbara will be curated by comedian-actor Steve Martin and former Hammer Museum director Ann Philbin.

entry.title: EU orders Meta to restore WhatsApp access for rival AI chatbots
entry.content: European Union regulators have ordered Meta Platforms to restore access to WhatsApp for rival AI chatbot makers

entry.title: Over $500,000 worth of bourbon stolen in daylight warehouse heist
entry.content: Police are looking for a gang of thieves who stole more than 10,000 bottles of bourbon from a Philadelphia warehouse.

entry.title: MIT researchers channel AI to turn hand gestures into robot training data
entry.content: Humanoid robots struggling with tasks like grasping a cup have a new teacher — a person wearing an ultrasound wristband that captures the movement of muscles, tendons and ligaments beneath the skin

entry.title: This rural California county’s problem is all too common: how to reduce suicides when everyone has a gun
entry.content: Shasta county has one of the state’s highest rates of suicide and gun ownership. Here’s how locals are trying to combat itLike many men in the mountainous California county of Shasta, about 200 miles north of San Francisco, Bill Rocha loved to hunt and fish, spending the infernal summers out on the lake in his boat. For decades he made his living as a contractor, working hard with his hands every day. And like many men in rural parts of the state, Bill was a gun owner. He had several hunting rifles, some of which he kept locked in a safe, and another firearm that he kept unlocked in his car.Kelly Rocha, his daughter, described him as extremely sociable, but in private things were starting to fray. She didn’t know the extent of what her father was struggling with until she got a call one night in 2019. She had slept through two voicemails from her father’s wife, but finally picked up when her own mother called. “It was after midnight,” recalled Kelly, who was then 43. “She told me that my dad went out to his truck and killed himself.” Continue reading...

entry.title: Federal appeals court says Alabama nitrogen gas execution inflicts unconstitutional suffering
entry.content: A federal appeals court ruled Monday that Alabama's nitrogen gas execution method likely inflicts a cruel and constitutionally impermissible degree of suffering on condemned inmates -- but stopped short of blocking a scheduled execution set for Thursday.

entry.title: Whey protein shortage looms as use of weight-loss drugs fuels global demand
entry.content: Price of dairy product has risen fivefold after users of GLP-1 medications advised to increase protein consumptionThe growing popularity of weight-loss drugs has fuelled global demand for whey protein, sparking concerns among industry experts over a potential shortage.The price of whey has risen fivefold to record levels as companies race to secure supplies amid a boom driven by growing use of GLP-1 drugs, such as Mounjaro, which often require higher protein intake to preserve muscle mass. Continue reading...

entry.title: Which Stock Sectors Do Best When Interest Rates Are Rising, Falling or Flat?
entry.content: Research finds a flat environment is best for stocks of all stripes, but there are some surprising results when the Fed is cutting or raising rates.

entry.title: Apple and Brussels blame each other for delaying European Union rollout of Siri AI
entry.content: Apple and the European Union are blaming each other for delaying the rollout of Apple's Siri AI app to European users

entry.title: ‘Woefully unprepared’: extreme heat will double US hospitalizations by 2040, study finds
entry.content: Sharp rise in hospital visits will in turn drive up annual healthcare costs for heat-related conditions to over $1bnPeople in the US are poised to endure another summer of unusually ferocious heat and there will be little respite in the years ahead, with a new study finding that the coming 15 years could see a doubling in hospitalizations due to heat-related illnesses.The number of annual heat-related emergency department visits or hospitalizations across the US are set to rise from about 109,000 cases a year to as many as 237,000 cases by 2040, the new research has estimated. Continue reading...

entry.title: Wyoming’s Data-Center Boom Meets the ‘Man Camp’ Backlash
entry.content: In America’s least populous state, neighbors are in an uproar over plans to build a small city to house thousands of out-of-state workers.

entry.title: Drug suspects arrested after vehicle in high-speed chase hits pedestrians
entry.content: One suspect drove a vehicle "at very high speed and recklessly, even hitting several local residents" before being stopped by authorities, officials said.

entry.title: Teen charged over London arson attack on Jewish charity ambulances
entry.content: 18-year-old Subhan Ahmed allegedly assisted with the torching of four ambulances used by a Jewish volunteer organization

entry.title: Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to "stop 'shooting'"
entry.content: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.

entry.title: Trump says Iran deal could be signed in 2 or 3 days
entry.content: Trump voices optimism for a "very good deal" with Iran in just a few days, as a tense truce between Israel and Iran appears to hold.

entry.title: 2 pilots killed in plane crash en route to pick up former MLB star
entry.content: MLB All-Star former catcher Yadier Molina said on social media that the plane was bound for Texas to pick him up, along with family and friends.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama, Spurs send boisterous Knicks fans home quiet with Game 3 win
entry.content: New York Knicks fans rained vulgarities down on Victor Wembanayma and his teammates inside Madison Square Garden but the San Antonio Spurs responded with their best game of the NBA Finals.
